title: cyperus karisimbiensis
intro: Cyperus karisimbiensis is a rare and endemic species of sedge, belonging to the family Cyperaceae, found in the high-altitude wetlands of the Virunga Mountains in Rwanda. The plant has been traditionally used in Rwandan medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Cyperus karisimbiensis include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent to relieve pain and swelling. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in water to be consumed orally, and it can also be used topically as a poultice to speed up wound healing and reduce inflammation.
